pattern: A bitmask DP over the set of already-placed nodes assigns the next position to any node whose predecessors are all placed, maximizing the score-times-position sum.
hints: The position a node gets is one more than the number of nodes placed before it, so it only depends on how many are already chosen.
Track which subset of nodes has been placed; a node is eligible next only if every node with an edge into it is already in the subset.

Let dp[mask] be the best profit having placed exactly the nodes in mask. For each eligible next node v (its predecessor mask is a subset of mask), the new position is popcount(mask)+1; update dp[mask | (1<<v)].

edge_inputs.description: single chain forces the only order
one root with two free successors, higher score goes last
no edges, biggest score should take the last position

stress.description: random DAG on up to 12 nodes with edges only from lower to higher index
stress.note: kept to <=12 nodes so the 2^n DP stays fast
